text:
Chapter 3: The Plant Cell

3.1 Cell Wall
The plant cell wall is composed of cellulose microfibrils. It provides structural support and protection.

The cell membrane lies just inside the cell wall. It is selectively permeable, controlling what enters and exits.

The cytoplasm contains various organelles including:
- Chloroplasts (photosynthesis)
- Mitochondria (cellular respiration)
- Ribosomes (protein synthesis)
- Endoplasmic reticulum (transport)

diagrams.label: Plant Cell Diagram
diagrams.description: A simplified, two-dimensional cross-section diagram representing a typical plant cell. The most prominent feature is a large, central, circular structure labeled 'Nucleus'. This nucleus is contained within the cell's boundaries, which are implied by the surrounding text describing the cell wall and membrane.

captions: Fig. 3.1: A typical plant cell showing major organelles.
